assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 87-88: 'Assertion change in `test_recommend_purls_basic`: The base-branch assertion checked `body.items[0].purl` against a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers (`"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar"`). The PR-branch assertion checks against a versioned PURL without qualifiers (`"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12"`). While two new `assert!(!...contains("?"))` assertions were added (additive), the primary value assertion was relaxed from a longer, more specific expected value to a shorter one -- REDUCTIVE (assertion relaxation)'

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 66: 'The classification is based on comparing the base-branch and PR-branch content of test files, independent of task requirements.' Lines 72-76 enumerate base-branch functions; lines 78-83 enumerate PR-branch functions. Line 87 compares base-branch and PR-branch assertion values ('The base-branch assertion checked... The PR-branch assertion checks...'). The structural analysis references specific file content (function names, assertion strings) rather than acceptance criteria. The semantic assessment (lines 98-103) discusses test coverage implications, not task requirements.
